mtrl.desc: qCFW - Arrives to Slim (2500-3000) & SuperSlim (4000-NOR) PlayStation 3 Models

br.div: UPDATE (Feb. 20) -PS3HEN 3.4.2 Open Beta: Test #1 Released- (Forum Link)UPDATE (Feb. 20) -qCFW updated(forum link) --qcfw-20260220-release(github link)UPDATE (Feb. 17) -qCFW and Future potential(Forum Thread)by@aomsin2526>here<UPDATE (Feb. 13) -BadWDSD/qCFW + HW Flasher/NoBT(Forum Thread)UPDATE (Feb. 11) -qCFW files have been temporary removed from github by the developer, due to a bug, see@aomsin2526's post >here< & >also here<UPDATE (Feb. 3)-PS3HEN v3.4.1 (STABLE) has now been releasedby@esc0rtd3w, graduating from the 3.4.1 BETA builds. (Note: v3.4.1+ is required for qCFW)​

mtrl.desc: [BD-JB-1250] New Exploit (bluray) for PlayStation 4 supporting upto 12.50 firmware from Gezine

br.div: The PlayStation 4 has new BluRay exploit that supports upto 12.50 Firmware. Thanks to the efforts of developerGezine,who has released the source code and helloworld.iso to complete the proof of work of this recent exploit for the PS4. Below you can see the readme for the project. Also some community reaction and comments about the new exploit for the PS4 from Abkarino & Modded Warefare dives into the exploit in one of his latest video attached​

mtrl.desc: {CFW Release} 4.92.2 Evilnat Custom Firmware (w/ Cobra v8.5) - Includes: CEX/PEX/D-PEX) + noBD/BT

br.div: After a successful Open Beta for 4.92.2, PlayStation 3 CFW developer@Evilnathas nowOFFICIALLYupdated hisEvilnat Custom Firmware'sfor the PS3 to version4.92.2,A number of new changes and options have been added in this release for your PS3 FAT and Early Slim models capable of installing a CFW PUP.Various patches to the CFW have been made as well as various new additions to theCobra Payload, Also theXAI Pluginwas not left out, as there are some new options one being a newNew Overclock menuhas been introduced to the firmware. Now we no longer need a dedicated PUP to change overclock settings, it can be done directly on the XMB. All the details and changes can be found in Evilnat's release note's found below and also in the link provided at the bottom.​

mtrl.desc: ProtoPwn (Protokernel Exploit by pcm720) - Now all PS2s are hackable via MemoryCard (MILESTONE)

br.div: Breaking Newsin the PS2 Scene,as a NEW MemoryCard Exploit for Protokernel PS2s (SCPH-10000, SCPH-15000, and DTL-H10000(S)) just got released!!!It took 25 years to reach this point, butNOW ALL PS2s including ALL special models(Arcade, Prototype, Debug, Consumer-model, "special/custom builds")can load Homebrew WITHOUT DISCS!​

mtrl.desc: BadWDSD HW Exploit - With new powerful qCFW for PS3 Slim & SuperSlim NOR models on the horizon

br.div: Update (Jan. 30) -@esc0rtd3whas releaseda new beta, while not the FINAL (yet) it is the first beta HEN with the qCFW installer.Update (Jan. 29) -@aomsin2526has released thefirst public version of qCFWfor the BadWDSD exploit, now we still needPS3HEN 3.4.1 (FINAL)for the qCFW installer to utilize the qCFW release.Update (Jan. 15)-@aomsin2526has provided a new progress report,qCFWis nearing a public release.>>>LinkUpdate (Dec. 8) -@aomsin2526(aka chattrapat_s / kafuu)has provided an update for the project, in the form of a "To-Do" list for the project, with some reported progress like"qCFW is done"see full post>>> LinkUpdate (Sept. 11) -New video demonstration and details "qCFW will be based on Evilnat PEX from now on. This means both CEX and...​

mtrl.desc: PS3HEN w/ Linux & Overclocking Support? BadHTAB A hardware-software based hypervisor (lv1) exploit

br.div: UPDATE 4 - (May 2 / June 9):@esc0rtd3whas created a fork of the BadHTAB Exploit with some changes such as an "Automatic reboot if glitch crashes ps3" feature among other changes to make it easier for testing the new exploit.(June 9)- ReadMe Updated in link belowUPDATE 2 & 3:Now, not only Linux but also Overclocking on PS3HEN is now possible w/BadHTAB exploit&PS3HEN (beta builds)that are currently supporting the exploit. This is still considered an experimental exploit & features soUse at your Risk. (See update tabs/links) for additional details and information on these advancements for the PlayStation 3.Original Article: Could Linux Support onPS3HENbe a reality with new exploit? Yes, and also other features but itsnot going to be for everyone. There is a newProof of Conceptreleased for the PS3 that has been implemented byKafuu(@aomsin2526)usingGeohot'soldhtab glitchon PS3HEN. Developer@zecoxaofirst started posting about this idea and the concept in Jan. of 2023 here inthe forum'sand has shared various thoughts and progress, with the latest progress being Kafuu's implementation.BadHTABis ahardware-softwarebased hypervisor (lv1) exploit,The exploit has a very low success rate of (5-10%)as GameOS has a much smaller "glitch window". As the readme states this not meant "for daily driver, it is for user's withskill and patienceonly.".​

mtrl.desc: PlayStation Home Revived and Enhanced by Home Laboratory!

br.div: Home is back on the XMB in 2025! Download and Play just like it was originally!I am pleased to present our extensive work on reviving and enhancingPlayStation Home. Led by AgentDark447, our team has developed an advanced, open-source iteration of this server platform, significantly expanding its capabilities beyond the original. Below, I outline the project’s scope, technical advancements, and access instructions for those interested in exploring or contributing.​

mtrl.desc: PS3 Toolset (by bguerville) with 4.92 support has been released.

br.div: If you have a Custom Firmware compatible PlayStation 3 Model and are running 4.92 Official Firmware and need to Jailbreak for the first time or if you updated your previous CFW installation by mistake to 4.92 OFW. Then the PS3 Toolset will exploit your PS3 via the console's web browser and visitingps3toolset.com website(see tutorial tab), then once the process has completed you will be able to install a Custom Firmware (such as 4.92 Evilnat) once again or for the first time.Your PlayStation 3 model must be able to install a CFW, these would be all the FAT models PS3 and Early production Slim Models. The easiest and most effective ways to determine if your PS3 can install a cfw​

mtrl.desc: 4.92 Littlebalup's DUALBOOT firmware Released

br.div: Since the4.92 firmware updatethings have been flowing out from the community in response. We have seenPS3HEN (& hfw) releasedfor 4.92 and we have seenEvilnat updateand release his cfw collection on 4.92. Now developer@littlebalupdrops by the forumand release the latestDualBoot (DB) firmwareto support 4.92 firmware.This release for Custom Firmware user's is a very useful firmware, if you use PSN still and do not want to go online with a modded system and risk a potential ban. As it allows you to use an Official firmware that can re-install a CFW without having re-exploiting the console with the toolset or flash writer. See full details in @littlebalups release notes about the Dualboot firmware concept.​

mtrl.desc: 4.92 Evilnat Custom Firmware (w/ Cobra v8.5) Released - CEX,PEX,D-PEX,DEX builds

br.div: UPDATExai_plugin:New Overclocking Tools (change on the fly) / New ps2netemu improvements added.Following the4.92 firmware updatedeveloper's from around the community have been updating various projects. This time around PS3 CFW developer@Evilnatdrops by the forumand leave's us with a suite of ps3 custom firmware's that support 4.92 firmware. 4 Flavor's (CEX, PEX, D-PEX & DEX) are available for 4.92 Evilnat CFW, depending on your console/taste, all builds are equipped with theCobra v8.5 payloadto provide expanded feature set over Standard CFW's.If you are already on PS3 CFW, updating is a breeze, you just need to install the new CFW PUP file via the official USB Method and you will be on Evilnat's latest release. If you have updated to 4.92 OFW you will have to wait, as you will have re-exploit your ps3 console to allow for a Custom Firmware installation once again. The good news is that developer@bguervilleshould be rolling out a new update soon for the PS3 Toolset that support 4.92 firmware. The offline tool CFW Flash Writer may take longer for the community to update it, but we hope to see an update for that tool soon as well.​

mtrl.desc: PS3HEN 3.4.0 + HFW 4.92 - Officially released by esc0rtd3w of Team PS3Xploit

br.div: PS3 Developer@esc0rtd3whas released the latest update toPS3HENwith support to recent firmware updateof 4.92. Along with the PS3HEN update is the release ofHybrid Firmware (HFW), which is just an official firmware (4.92) with the "silk_webkit.sprx" file replace from 4.82 OFW (that has a vulnerability patched in 4.83 OFW, the last attempt Sony made to curb homebrew). HFW was created by@Joonieand has been apart of this 4.92 release of well.There have been a number of new changes in this update marked v3.4.0 as it does not just provide 4.92 support but various changes as seen in the changelog provided​

mtrl.desc: PS3 4.92 Official FIrmware has been released - PS3 CFW & PS3HEN DO NOT UPDATE to 4.92

br.div: As expectedthe PlayStation 3 has receivedfirmware update 4.92, if you are currently usingPS3HENorCFWyouSHOULD NOT updateyour PS3 Firmware to 4.92 at this time. Otherwise you will be temporary losing your jailbreak's and ability to run homebrew on the console, until developer's release new exploits/jailbreaks that support the new 4.92 firmware update. There isa processin which need to take place for things to catch up, as developer's@bguerville@esc0rtd3w@Evilnatand other's havebeen readyfor the update to launch to get to work quickly.We learned last week that@bguervillehas an update for the PS3 Toolset with new features being deployed as the developer explains in these two post >1<​

mtrl.desc: Update 4.91.3 > > > [PS3 CFW] Evilnat's 4.91.2 Custom Firmware's Collection Released

br.div: UPDATEJanuary 2025:CFW Evilnat 4.91.3[BETA 1] released>>Click Here<<December '24:Merry Christmas and Happy Holiday's to everyone, PS3 Custom firmware developer@Evilnathas dropped by the psx-place forum's on this Christmas day to present the final build(4.91.2)of the developer's ps3 Custom Firmware suite, after a series of Open Beta releases, v4.91.2 now officially drops the "BETA" tag and in its final form for 4.91.2. For discussion and full details about this PS3 firmware release and the various cfw types offered in this suite of releases, checkout the developer's release thread here in the forum's detailing all the various features and functions in Evilnat's latest releases notes and details for4.91 Evilnat's CFW (w/ Cobra v8.5).​

mtrl.desc: New PS1/PS2 Emulation Features for PS3 CFW user's + Evilnat's 4.91.2-BETA 12

br.div: UPDATE: January 2025:ps2gxemu (for CECH-C/E ps3 models) has been updated by@kozarovv+ netemu to gxemu config conversion tool by@Zarhas been released, see details at bottomNov. 2024: Recently we have had some great news with developments surroundingPS1 & PS2 Emulation on the PS3 for Custom Firmware user'sas the emulator's that reside in the PlayStation 3 Firmware have receivedsome modded tweaksthanks to@kozarovv&@mrjaredbeta, The files can be added manually to many firmware's or you can update to the latest release ofEvilnat's 4.91.2 CFW (BETA 12)that have these newly modded emulator's already installed and ready to go thanks to@Evilnat.The changes and feature's depend on the PS2/PS1 emulator being used and also on which model of the PS3 (with PS2). Below you will find a brief explanation of the emulator, mixed in with the readme details and some added screenshot. Some of the feature's need more testing to see how affective they may be such as the partial antiblur and EE Overclocking/Underclocking patches. View all the details and related links found below.​

mtrl.desc: CFW Flash Writer - Unofficial 4.91 update

br.div: Developer@aldostoolshas released an unofficial update for theCFW Flash Writerto supportHFW 4.91on the PS3. This can be a secondary candidate for allowing Custom Firmware installations whenbguerville's PS3Toolset (https://www.ps3toolset.com/bgtoolset/)isn't accessible or a self hosting offline option. However, ThePs3toolset is still the preferred and safest method when available for hacking your capable ps3 model so its ready for PS3 Custom Firmware installations.​

mtrl.desc: PPPwn - PlayStation 4 PPPoE RCE - (The First PS4 Kernel RCE - Supporting upto FW 11.00) by TheFlow

br.div: Scene Developer & HackerTheFlowwho has been a legend in various PlayStation Scene's, has done it yet again!! This time the developer has released "The first PlayStation 4 Kernel RCE, Supporting FWs upto 11.00".With the newly released (PoC) jailbreak the community will be able to update popular Homebrew Enabler's (Mira & GoldHEN) to support firmware 11.00 with this new jailbreak that TheFlow has disclosed (CVE-2006-4304) through the bounty program and has been patched in the latest PS4 firmware available​

mtrl.desc: Official PS3 Toolset Update (v1.2.007) Release (4.91 Support)

br.div: FollowingSony firmware update of 4.91for the PS3, developer's across the scene have been updating various exploits and tools. We have seen developer@Evilnatrelease his4.91 PS3 Custom Firmware'searlier in the week. Then we recently just had@esc0rtd3w&@JoonieprovidePS3HEN (& HFW) updated with 4.91 Support. Now we have developer@bguervillewith an update to hisPS3 Toolsetto support the exploiting of 4.91 OFW for custom firmware installation for capable models.​

mtrl.desc: Official PS3HEN v3.3.0 + HFW Released for 4.91 Firmware from Team PS3Xploit

br.div: TheOfficial PS3HENexploit has beenupdated to v3.3.0with some new changes from@esc0rtd3wand@JoonieofTeam PS3Xploitto support 4.91. Also, developer Joonie has brought back the webkit exploit from 4.82 to 4.91 with the official HFW release(HFW = OFW with old webkit injected). Its best to use the official HFW when using the official PS3HEN release.​

mtrl.desc: [Update Re-Release] PS3 Custom Firmware - 4.91 Evilnat Cobra (8.4) CFW Released

br.div: UPDATE -See 1st post for details, Re-Release a few bugs correctedPlayStation 3 CFW developer@Evilnathas just released his much anticipated4.91 Evilnat Custom Firmwarefollowingthe recent routine updatefrom Sony when they dropped firmware 4.91 last week. If you are on any Custom Firmware currently all you have to do is install this CFW PuP by the USB method. You will not need to wait for an update to bguerville's PS3 Toolset to exploit your system (as its already exploited if you are running CFW).If you had updated to 4.91 OFW or by mistake installed a HFW you will have to wait for the PS3 Toolset to be updated. Developerbguervillewas waiting for the first 4.91 CFW to drop before making his update and that has just occurred with this release, so it should not be to long before we see bguerville provide a 4.91 update to the toolset as the developerdescribed herein the psx-place forums..​

mtrl.desc: PS3 4.91 Firmware Released - Do Not Update!!!

br.div: UPDATE:its now safe to update to 4.91. PS3HEN (& HFW), CFW & PS3 Toolset have been updated to support 4.91.PS3 Firmware 4.91 is now LiveThe expected once a year firmware update has been executed by Sony, PS3 4.91 Firmware is now Live. While an expected update, could this bring other changes to the firmware? Likely not but its still early to conclude as this update is fresh as of writing .Do not update, various exploits / tools / essential homebrew will need to be updated to support the latest firmware update of 4.91. See below for a short FAQ on 4.91, as there is always some confusions around the update process. .​

mtrl.desc: PS3 Firmware 4.90 (OFW) is Live - Do Not Update, see full details!!!

br.div: As Sony tends to do, they like to come with a surprise firmware update when you least expect it and here they are with a surprise4.90 firmware update, now there has been some panic of potential changes and exploits that could be patched in 4.90, well that is lhe case and not a worry (you shouldn't update yet, but no exploits have been closed), Since firmware 4.80 there has not been any changes to the kernel, developer@Joonieconfirms in the forumsherethat4.90 use's the 4.80 kernel sourceand since firmware 4.83 we have not seen any counter measure to deter homebrew capabilities on the console for CFW or PS3HEN,Which that attempt (in 4.83) was countered by@Jooniewith the creation of HFW (OFW w/ 4.82 webkit injected). Since that time we have seen the towel thrown in as 4.84 / 4.85 / 4.86 / 4.87 /4.88 / 4.89 and now in 4.90. The update is the usual BluRay update and not much more, but good to see Sony still supporting the BluRay drive (as they should IMO).​

mtrl.desc: CFW RELEASED - 4.89.3 Evilnat (Cobra v8.4) Official debut of PEX (New PS3 CFW type) + Other flavor's

br.div: UPDATE (Re-Download PUP)-via@Evilnat"CFW 4.89.3 updated withCobra 8.4by default andxai_updater updated to v1.2for all versions! It fixes updater package for CEX and DEX."Developer@Evilnathas releasedthe much anticipated updateto his PS3 CFW with the release of4.89.3 Evilnat CFW.This update comes with a variety of PUP to choose from and as we beendetailing overthe pastseveral monthsthere are alot of new features in this custom firmware release from developer@Evilnat,This firmware brings back a lost relic of the scene that was stopped at firmware 4.84 and I am speaking ofRebug REX(the original hybrid of CEX(retail) / DEX (debug) firmware), With no DEX firmware available to the public past 4.84 there was a problem, developer's knew how to create a REX style firmware,they have just been missing the DEX portion since 4.84. Well developer@Evilnatdecided he would try and bring 4.84 DEX to 4.89 by porting the firmware and after some trials and error's the developer was able to achieve that goal and now we haveEvilnat PEX (& D-PEX) CFWwhich is a hybrid like Rebug REX..​

mtrl.desc: Evilnat 4.89.3 CFW (Official Release - Coming Soon) PEX / OverClocking / Cobra v8.4 / xai_plugin /.

br.div: As we have been telling yousince last Octoberdeveloper@Evilnathas been working on an epic PS3 Custom Firmware (CFW) with the upcoming release of4.89.3 Evilnat CFW. Back in October we weredetailing someof the changes coming to the custom xai_plugin that, The custom changes provides many advance features at your fingertips right from the convenience of the XMB, Since that time the developer has added more feature's to the xai_plugin, a plugin that comes embedded in Evilnat's firmware's. On his personal twitter the developer has detailed some of those new functions also many of those can be seen in the official discussion thread for 4.89 Evilnat CFWhereas well.​

mtrl.desc: PS3 Dynarec support on PicoDrive (Sega 32x & Improved Sega CD) on the horizon from OsirisX

br.div: Good news if your a fan of retro emulation and have a jailbroken PS3, as developer@OsirisXhas made a bit of a breakthrough for emulation on the PS3. Following OsirisX other emulation release's / contributions like theunofficial PS4 port of RetroArchand the progress of theRetroArch Psl1ght PS3 portThe developer is now tackling taking on a new adventure on the PS3.One of the issues on the PS3, has always been the supporting of later generations(the 3d era+)of emulation. While we have seen PS1 / PSP / PS2 emulated on the PS3, they are using the unlocked system's firmware emulator's from Sony,. homebrew emulation has not achieved the same success, without support of a dynarec and the emulators re-written to support such a dynarec on the PS3, user's have been stuck side-scrolling in their Retro experiances. We seen an early example years ago when developer IngPeria released a PoC PS1 emulator (not Sony's firmware one) with dynarec support, but not many other developer's took on the task for any other emulators.​

mtrl.desc: PlayStation Home v0.41 (GDC Demo 2007) playable on HEN & CFW from DeViL303

br.div: PlayStation 3 developer@DeViL303has been working on a new project of late, taking a break from some of the fantastic XMB work and various firmware hacks and tweaks. The developer has recently set his sights on PlayStation Home and bringing some of the functionality back while preserving some of the history of the project. DeViL303 has managed to get alot working in the GDC Demo 2007 of Home. There are various notes and details provided below along with a video demonstration of what is working. See full details in the release thread linked​

mtrl.desc: [UPDATE x5] What is Evilnat brewing up in his next PS3 CFW Release? 4.89.3 in development

br.div: PlayStation 3 developer@Evilnathas been researching and developing some new features for his popularEvilnat's Cobra CFWfor the PS3. Sony may have dropped official updates, but the development community has not stopped support and continues to pack new features and functionality directly into the firmware. With 4.89.3now confirmedin development by@EvilnatSome of these features are still in the PoC stage and some should be implemented in the next stable release (once fully tested) of the CFW. Currently the community developer's only have access to 4.84 DEX firmware, so the developer is trying to find (if it exist) 4.89 DEX (or a higher then 4.84 DEX) but the developer seems to be experimenting with "porting 4.84 DEX Kernel and required modules to 4.89 (like Rebug REX/D-REX) and is working as expected until now". However the developer warns at this time its still needs alot of testing but its encouraging to see some great development still going into PlayStation 3 CFW and to the Cobra Payload.​

mtrl.desc: OFFICIAL bguerville's PS3 Toolset v1.2.004 Update Now Live - Supporting 4.89 + NEW Features

br.div: Notice OFFICIAL PS3 Toolset is not currently online, will be back soon.See Link for more info:https://www.psx-place.com/threads/4...irmware-capable-models-status-warnings.38553/Beware there are some unofficial versions out there in the wild, They are incomplete and have a high brick risk they should not be consider mirror. When official PS3 Toolset is back online we will update you here,.Following the firmware update of 4.89 the scene was awaiting an update tobguerville's PS3 Toolsetso that CFW capable console who may of updated to 4.89 (or jailbreaking for the first time) can run the exploit and once again and be able to install CFW from 4.89. Now for those experienced CFW user's they knew they should wait and NOT UPDATE when a new firmware update goes live as those user's simply just waited for Evilnat's 4.89 Cobra CFW to be released. .Now back to the official toolset update, behind the scene's prior to the 4.89 update the v1.2 update was near deployment. So instead of making various updates, one to support 4.89 and one for v1.2 new feature's it made sense for the project to have one testing session and release in a single update for 4.89 but that meant it a bit of delay for the deployment since proper testing needed to be conducted with v1.2 features. However in that time period a Russian Scene site had found someone else's re-implementation(they forget to mention that)and patched worked together a toolset that looked like the real thing but it was hardly bguerville's original toolset nor did it offered the safety/quality or feature set of the real release.There have been various changes in v1.2004 but the much anticipatedFile Manager&xRegistry Editorwill still be rolled out in a future update that will arrive soon after some theming issue's are sorted. To quote developer@bguervilleabout the new features in this update the developer states "there is a few new features to play with other than 4.89 support, like some memory patching options including HAN, memory dumping, extended system information details and syscon error logs. Afaik the issue that led to crashes on page load on certain setups should be fixed" Additional details can be seen in the tabs below .​

mtrl.desc: (UPDATE 2 ) Thefl0w discloses"Blu-ray Disc Java Sandbox Escape" vulnerabilities (PS3, PS4, PS5)

br.div: Update (See Tabs Below PS3 / PS4):Since the disclosure several developer's have went on the hunt and we have seen PoC releases and TEST payload pushed, We have confirmation the PoC is working on 9.00, other have been testing impact on 9.03/.04. See all the details from developer's likesleirsgoevy /@zecoxao/ psxdev(aka bigboss)in the PS4 Tab. Some preliminary discussions about the PS3 impact have been discussed in this thread from various developer's like@bguervilleand@zecoxaoOriginal Article:DeveloperTheFl0wwho is well known in the Homebrew and Hacking in the PlayStation Community, via the hackerone program the hacker has yet again disclosed details on vulnerabilities (5) "bd-jb Blu-ray Disc Java Sandbox Escape" is affecting PS3, PS4, PS5.Sony has patched these vulnerabilities in recent firmware updates for the PS5 / PS4 with firmware update's of 5.00 FW for the PS5 and 9.50 FW for the PS4. These methods are also likely to work on the PS3 according to thefl0w (likely on the latest firmware).thefl0w has detailed some advantages this concept provides over a webkit exploit, you can see that information and various details in the "additional info" tab below, along with some clarification due to over hyping some aspects of this exploit..:​

mtrl.desc: RetroArch PS4 (OrbisDev Port) official release on the horizon (PS4 support added to mainline Repo)

br.div: If you follow thepsx-place twitter feed, you may of seen aretweet from libretro's officialaccount yesterday, announcing theRetroArch PS4code being merged and adopted into the mainline RetroArch code repo, this mean soon we could see the official release of RetroArch PS4, as the official team has accepted theOrbisdev Portsubmission from scene developer's . For sometime there has been an unofficial port ofRetroArch PS4by developer@OsirisX,who (unrelated to ps4) also shared recently his progress on thepsl1ght port for the PS3 hereforums, to help bring the PS3 back into the mainline RetroArch repo. As the official team removed the old PS3 port in favor of the Open Source psl1ght SDK.The same thing was happening with the PS4, while we had a very functionalunofficial RetroArch PS4 portas seen here in the forums by the dev, it was never adopted in the official project's mainline repo, because the official team preferred aOrbisDev (OpenSource SDK)PS4 port. Thanks to various ps4 developer's like@fjtrujy/@OsirisX/ @frangar&@psxdev. We now have a build that is close to an official release created with the OpenOrbis SDK.​

mtrl.desc: PS3HEN v3.1.1 & HFW Officially updated with 4.89 Support - (The PS3 Exploit for nonCFW PS3 Models)

br.div: UPDATE: v3.1.1 released with HOTFIX see changelog below for v3.1.1Following thefirmware update of 4.89that Sony rolled out last week, the reunion of ps3 developer's was set for the PS3 homebrew / hacking community, First we seen well known Ps3 Homebrew / CFW developer@Evilnatdelivered his4.89 Evilnat (Cobra 8.3)a few days ago for those models that can install a PS3 Custom Firmware, but for those console that can not you can get many CFW features: like the ability to launch homebrew and plugins with the PS3HEN exploit. As@esc0rtd3wof thePS3Xploit Teamhas just released PS3HEN and fellow scene developer@littlebaluphas delivered the special firmware (HFW) that restore the exploits from firmware 4.82 that allows for the execution of PS3HEN.In this official release of PS3HEN v3.1.0(& HFW)for 4.89 firmware, there has been various changes to PS3HEN from developer@esc0rtd3wand various scene developer's like@bucanero,@sandungasand also@xps3rimentshave contributed to as detailed in the changelogs for this latest release of PS3HEN (Official).​
